scenes.title: The Road to Redstone
content: You have heard the name Redstone spoken in low voices — at waystation fires, in market stalls, by merchants who turned their carts the other direction. An iron town carved into the valley's edge, once rich, now something else. Caravans go missing. The roads grow quiet. But the posting boards still carry coin, and coin does not ask for courage.

scenes.title: Shadows on the Edge of Town
content: Redstone lies below — smoke from chimneys, lanterns dim in the early dark, a crooked church steeple rising from the swamp beyond the south wall. A man pulls his door shut before you reach the end of his street. The gate guards wave you through without a word. No questions. No greeting. Just eyes that look past you, searching the treeline for something they do not name.

scenes.title: Arrival at the Tavern
content: The Broken Blade sits at the center of town like a coal that has not gone cold. Inside: low voices, tallow smoke, the smell of ale gone warm. Heads turn when you push through the door, then turn away. Somewhere beneath the silence, Redstone has a story — and stories always find their start at a tavern table. You have made worse beginnings.
